title: Decidability and Undecidability in Theory of Computation
description: A flowchart illustrating the hierarchy of decidability problems, showing which problems are decidable by Turing Machines and which are undecidable, leading to examples like the Halting Problem.
relationships: Halting Problem is a canonical example of an Undecidable Problem
Rice's Theorem generalizes undecidability to non-trivial semantic properties
